titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph Slevin to the Longford Urban District Council, 4 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph Slevin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Joseph Slevin to the Longford Urban District Council in which Slevin submits his Towns Surveyor report.Joseph Slevin (b. 1856) was the Town Surveyor for Longford. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council Co. Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Town Surveyor Reported Mr. Chairman & Gentlemen As requested I inspected the ruins in
Ballymacormick graveyard and I consider it
would take Â£18 to repair it. We have had considerable trouble from time
to time claiming the drain pipe at the back of
your houses on St. Nicls Road. I asked on a
previous occasion to have the tenants noticed to
be particular as to what they put into it. I had
to get a new sink trap to replace one taken away
and slates and ridge tiles have been broken on some
of the privies. Dr Mayne reported a case of
fever on 8th Joseph's Road. The family have been
isolated and the place disinfected. Your obdt Servant Joseph Slevin 4th July 1916
